'''Owen Trout''' (born 15 October 1999) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf|sr}} or {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He has previously played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

Trout was called in to the [[England national rugby league team|England]] squad for the first time ahead of the Ashes in 2026.

==Background==
Owen Trout is a rugby league player, brother of the rugby league footballer; [[Kyle Trout]].

==Career==
===Leeds Rhinos===
In 2019 he made professional debut on loan at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. 

Later in 2019 he made his debut for Leeds Rhinos against Workington Town in the [[Challenge Cup|Challenge cup]].{{citation needed|date=April 2024}}

===Huddersfield Giants===
Trout made a move to  [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in 2020 playing six games.  

In 2021 he played 15 games.  

On 28 May 2022, Trout played for Huddersfield in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] loss to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-player-ratings-final-24089809/|title=Challenge Cup player ratings as numerous stars shine in enthralling contest|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk|access-date=29 May 2022|archive-date=3 June 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220603014903/https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-player-ratings-final-24089809|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Leigh Leopards===
On 13 October 2023 it was reported that he had signed a three-year deal to join [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=13 Oct 2023|title=Leigh Leopards catch second new signing of the day as England Knights international signs long-term deal|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-leopards-catch-second-new-signing-of-the-day-as-england-knights-international-signs-long-term-deal}}</ref>
Trout played in nearly every match for Leigh in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish fifth on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live-blog/12204/13227859/super-league-semi-finals-live-wigan-face-leigh-for-grand-final-spot/|title=Super League semi-finals: Wigan Warriors cruise past Leigh Leopards- live commentary and video clips|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

Trout played 27 games for Leigh in the 2025 Super League season including their semi-final loss to rivals Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/3-key-areas-that-saw-leigh-leopards-miss-out-on-old-trafford-as-grand-final-opportunity-passed-up/|title=3 key areas that saw Leigh Leopards miss out on Old Trafford as Grand Final opportunity passed up|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

===Cronulla Sharks===

On 26 March 2026, Trout was announced to have signed<ref>{{Cite web |date=2026-03-26 |title=Sharks snap up Trout as Englishman signs two-year deal |url=https://www.sharks.com.au/news/2026/03/26/sharks-reel-in-trout-as-englishman-signs-two-year-deal/ |access-date=2026-03-26 |website=Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks |language=en}}</ref> a two-year deal with the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla Sharks]] of the [[National Rugby League]] from the 2027 season.
